name: 2000s Movies
description: Cinema of the 2000s — blockbusters, Best Picture winners, and defining franchises

questions.q: Which 2009 James Cameron film about the Na'vi on the moon Pandora became the highest-grossing film ever made?

source.notes: Grossed over $2.9 billion worldwide across its theatrical runs.

questions.q: Which 2000 Ridley Scott historical epic starring Russell Crowe won Best Picture?

source.notes: Crowe won Best Actor as Maximus.

questions.q: Heath Ledger won a posthumous Oscar for playing The Joker in which 2008 Christopher Nolan film?

source.notes: Ledger died in January 2008, months before the film's release.

questions.q: The 2001 DreamWorks animated film starring an ogre voiced by Mike Myers was called what?

source.notes: Won the first-ever Academy Award for Best Animated Feature.

questions.q: Which 2003 Pirates of the Caribbean film kicked off the franchise?
choices: The Curse of the Black Pearl

source.original_a: Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl
source.notes: Directed by Gore Verbinski, starring Johnny Depp and Orlando Bloom.

questions.q: Which 2008 Marvel film starring Robert Downey Jr. launched the Marvel Cinematic Universe?

source.notes: Directed by Jon Favreau; released May 2, 2008.

questions.q: Which Peter Jackson trilogy dominated the early 2000s, with its final chapter winning 11 Oscars?

source.notes: Return of the King (2003) tied the all-time Oscar record with 11 wins.

questions.q: In Pixar's 2003 film Finding Nemo, what's the name of Nemo's father who crosses the ocean to find him?

source.notes: Voiced by Albert Brooks; the film won Best Animated Feature.

questions.q: Which 2001 Harry Potter movie (titled Philosopher's Stone outside the US) was the first in the series?
choices: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire

source.original_a: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
source.notes: Released November 2001; directed by Chris Columbus.

questions.q: Which 2006 Martin Scorsese crime drama set in Boston finally won him Best Director?

source.notes: Remake of the Hong Kong film Infernal Affairs; also won Best Picture.

questions.q: Sandra Bullock won Best Actress for which 2009 sports drama based on the true story of Michael Oher?

source.notes: Bullock's first Oscar win; the film did not win Best Picture.

questions.q: Which 2008 Danny Boyle film about a Mumbai slum kid on a game show won Best Picture?

source.notes: Won 8 Oscars including Best Director and Best Picture.

questions.q: Daniel Day-Lewis won Best Actor for which 2007 Paul Thomas Anderson oil-tycoon drama?

source.notes: Day-Lewis plays oilman Daniel Plainview; famous 'milkshake' scene.

questions.q: Which 2004 Brad Bird-directed Pixar film about a family of superheroes won Best Animated Feature?

source.notes: Also won Best Sound Editing; sequel released in 2018.

questions.q: Which 2002 Rob Marshall-directed musical starring Renée Zellweger and Catherine Zeta-Jones won Best Picture?

source.notes: First musical to win Best Picture since Oliver! in 1968.

questions.q: Which 2001 Baz Luhrmann film starring Nicole Kidman and Ewan McGregor revived the movie musical?

source.notes: Set in 1899 Paris; jukebox score built on pop hits.

questions.q: Which 2000 Christopher Nolan film features a protagonist with anterograde amnesia and a reverse-chronological structure?

source.notes: Guy Pearce stars; Nolan's breakout feature.

questions.q: Which 2008 Pixar film about a garbage-compacting robot won Best Animated Feature?

source.notes: Directed by Andrew Stanton; set 700 years in the future.

questions.q: Which 2004 Clint Eastwood boxing drama starring Hilary Swank won Best Picture?

source.notes: Swank won Best Actress; Morgan Freeman won Best Supporting Actor.

questions.q: Which 2007 Coen Brothers film starring Javier Bardem as Anton Chigurh won Best Picture?

source.notes: Adapted from Cormac McCarthy's novel; Bardem won Best Supporting Actor.

questions.q: Which 2004 Michel Gondry romance stars Jim Carrey and Kate Winslet trying to erase each other from memory?
choices: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

source.original_a: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
source.notes: Charlie Kaufman won Best Original Screenplay.

questions.q: Which 2006 Guillermo del Toro dark fantasy set during post-Civil War Spain won three Oscars?

source.notes: Won Cinematography, Makeup, and Art Direction; Spanish-language film.
